text:
*[Bug 19011] New: Fix for cdplot returning probabilities greater than One*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19011>

            Bug ID: 19011
           Summary: Fix for cdplot returning probabilities greater than
                    One
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Graphics
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:christoph.dalitz@hs-niederrhein.de|christoph.dalitz@hs-niederrhein.de>

Created attachment 3614
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3614&action=edit>
demonstration and explantion of bug and its fix

When `cdplot` is applied to data with regions of low density, it may happen
that it returns probabilities greater than one, `Inf` or `NaN` values. After a
closer look at this problem, I have noticed that the comutation of values > 1
can be avoided by not computing the total (unconditional) density directly, but
by using the law of total probability instead. Moreover, NaN values can be
circumvented bye removing before constructing the returned functions with
`approxfun`.

The attached TAR archive contains the following files for reproducing the bug,
demonstrating the fix and for fixing the code:

- *cdplot-bug.pdf* describes the problem and how it is solved
- *cdplot-example.r* contains an example script demonstrating the problem and
the fix
- *cdplot-fix.r* implements the fix as a standalone script
- *cdplot.patch* is for patching *cdplot.R* in R 4.5.2 with

    patch src/library/graphics/R/cdplot.R cdplot.patch
.

I would greatly appreciate, if this patch were included in the next R release.

Christoph Dalitz

text:
*[Bug 19012] New: foreign::write.foreign() generates incorrect SAS INPUT statement for character variables*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19012>

            Bug ID: 19012
           Summary: foreign::write.foreign() generates incorrect SAS INPUT
                    statement for character variables
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: x86_64/x64/amd64 (64-bit)
               URL: <https://github.com/cornball-ai/rformat-lab/blob/main/f>
                    oreign_sas_bug.md
                OS: Linux-Ubuntu
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P3
         Component: I/O
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:troy@cornball.ai|troy@cornball.ai>

The SAS export generated by foreign::write.foreign(package = "SAS")
produces an incorrect INPUT statement when character variables are
not the final columns in a data frame.

Minimal example:

    df <- data.frame(
        a = c("x","y"),
        b = 1:2,
        c = c("p","q")
    )

    write.foreign(df, "df.txt", "df.sas", package = "SAS")

Observed OUTPUT (excerpt):

    INPUT
    a
    b
    c $ ;

Expected OUTPUT:

    INPUT
    a $
    b
    c $ ;

According to SAS DATA step documentation, character variables in
list input must be followed by a `$` modifier; otherwise they are
interpreted as numeric variables.

Investigation shows this is a regression introduced between
foreign 0.8-29 and 0.8-30 (Dec 2008), corresponding to SVN
revision r5283, where braces were removed from the loop generating
the INPUT statement, altering control flow.

The behavior in 0.8-29 is correct.

Proposed patch restores the previous loop structure:

--- writeForeignSAS.R
+++ writeForeignSAS.R
@@
-    for(v in 1L:ncol(df))
-        cat("\n", varnames[v], file = codefile, append = TRUE)
-        if(strings[v]) cat(" $ ", file = codefile, append = TRUE)
+    for(v in 1L:ncol(df)) {
+        cat("\n", varnames[v], file = codefile, append = TRUE)
+        if(strings[v])
+            cat(" $ ", file = codefile, append = TRUE)
+    }

Tested with current CRAN version of foreign; patch restores
expected SAS syntax without affecting other output.
